root_text.sn55.33:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, ariyasāvako buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgato hoti—
root_text.sn55.33:1.4: itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti.
root_text.sn55.33:3.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, ariyasāvako paññavā hoti udayatthagāminiyā paññāya samannāgato ariyāya nibbedhikāya sammā dukkhakkhayagāminiyā.
root_text.sn55.33:3.3: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cattāro puññābhisandā kusalābhisandā sukhassāhārā”ti.

translation_text.sn55.33:1.1: “Mendicants, there are these four kinds of overflowing merit, overflowing goodness that nurture happiness.
translation_text.sn55.33:1.3: It’s when a noble disciple has experiential confidence in the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.33:3.1: Furthermore, a noble disciple is wise. They have the wisdom of arising and passing away which is noble, penetrative, and leads to the complete ending of suffering.
translation_text.sn55.33:3.2: This is the fourth kind of overflowing merit, overflowing goodness that nurtures happiness.
translation_text.sn55.33:3.3: These are the four kinds of overflowing merit, overflowing goodness that nurture happiness.”
